summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a high‑energy 1972 show highlighted by a scorching Bertha opener and stellar Pigpen‑led jams.
review: The recording is praised as an “another gem” with crisp, well‑balanced soundboard quality that lets listeners hear every vocal riff and banter. Musically the night shines on the explosive Bertha opener, a twangy Black Throated Sunshine, a fire‑filled China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider, and one of the best “Looks Like Rain” performances of the era, all driven by Pigpen’s charismatic presence. A few less‑essential cuts (Me & Bobby, Mr. Charlie) are noted, but overall the show is considered a standout Pigpen‑era concert.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently enthusiastic 5‑star comments about both performance and soundboard quality; only one neutral/low‑rating note, giving a strong overall impression of an excellent show with superb audio.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers describe the tape as crisp, well‑balanced and “another gem”; banter is clear enough to hear Phil & Bobby’s Ghoul intro, indicating a clean soundboard feed with minimal house mic bleed.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set builds with a hot Bertha opener and tight vocal harmonies, while the second set feels like an extended first‑set vibe—Pigpen drives the energy, the band stays confident in Dark Star/Other One, and the show ends on a high note with classic crowd‑pleasers.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Keeps lyrics together despite “good trouble,” adds extra twang to Black Throated Sunshine, contributes strong backing vocals.
band_member_comments.Phil: Prominent backing vocals on China>Rider; piano interjects in jams; highlighted by the “LET PHIL SING” crowd moment.
band_member_comments.Bob: Delivers solid story‑song vocals (e.g., Bertha), perfect harmonies on several numbers, and strong lead on Big Boss Man.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s piano adds flavor to the Good Lovin' jam; overall keyboard presence noted as more restrained post‑Europe ’72.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill’s drumming noted for a “squeaky‑spring” texture on Sugar Magnolia and an anticipated juicy solo in Good Lovin'.
